content.full_text:
DIRECTORS
Donald A. Bedard
PRESIDENT

Thomas A. Meadows
Barbara K. Northrop
Thomas F. Harrison
William C. Chileott

TRUCKEE TAHOE AIRPORT DISTRICT

Mark B. Foxwell
MANAGER

Ruth E. Geresy
ADMINISTRATIVE ASSISTANT

Patrick Kearney
MAINTENANCE SUPERVISOR

Barbara R. Laurie
COMPTROLLER

-In the heart of
America's All-year playground

Dec. 28, 1992

Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J St., Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Dear Sirs:

I am writing to the FPPC Legal Division requesting advice regarding a
possible conflict of interest which I may have. The following information is
pertinent to my situation:

a) 12/15/82     I learned by flight instructor certificate

b) 8/86          I retired from the USNavy and established permanent
                 residency in Truckee, CA

c) 11/87         I was elected to the Truckee Tahoe Airport District (TTAD)
                 Board of Directors

d) 12/15/88      I briefed the TTAD board of my intent to do flight instruction
                 for hire at the Truckee Tahoe Airport

e) A short discussion of Fixed Base Operators (FBO) is in order:

1) Todd Aero is an FBO tenant of Truckee Tahoe Airport. The lease
   permits aircraft maintenance, airplane rental, flight instruction, pilot
   supplies sales and snack food sales.

2) Sierra Warbirds, Inc. is an FBO tenant of Truckee Tahoe Airport. The
   lease permits aircraft maintenance, aircraft sales and instruction.

3) Pilot Services Corporation is an FBO tenant of Truckee Tahoe airport.
   the lease permits airplane rental, air taxi operations, flight instruction,
   pilot supply sales, and aerial photography.

4) The three FBOs are in competition with each other to a degree: i.e.
   Todd Aero and Sierra Warbirds, Inc. perform aircraft maintenance; and
   Todd Aero and Pilot Services Corporation offer flight instruction and
   aircraft rental. Each FBO has a written lease with TTAD. The FBOs
   occasionally come before the TTAD board for lease modifications and
   renewals and for expansion project plans approval/disapproval.

10356 Truckee Tahoe Airport Road • Truckee, California 96161 • Telephone (916) 587-4119 • FAX (916) 587-2984

f) 3/15/89          I did my first instructional (for hire) flight in a Todd Aero
airplane. The relationship between Todd Aero, the
customer (student) and me is that the customer rents the
airplane from Todd Aero and I work for the customer. Todd
Aero and I do not have a written agreement regarding my
doing flight instruction or scenic flights in Todd Aero aircraft.
Todd Aero does refer students to me and the other three
instructors who work with Todd Aero. Todd Aero does not
receive any compensation from students for flight instruction
provided to them. I do not enter into a written agreement
with a student. When a student and I fly together we use
Todd Aero aircraft, however on rare occasion I have taken a
student for a lesson in a Pilot Services Corporation aircraft.
Students rent the aircraft almost strictly from Todd Aero in
order to avoid confusion of flying a variety of aircraft. I also
do instruction in students' personal aircraft, and certified
pilots' personal aircraft.

g) My relationship with the FBOs is as follows:
     1) Todd Aero: I use Todd Aero space to work with students. I use a
drawer in a filing cabinet to keep aviation materials. I pay Todd Aero no
money for using the facilities. Although I have no regular hours of time
spent at Todd Aero, while I am there I answer the telephone, assist
customers, and do minor cleaning of the spaces. Todd Aero does not
provide me with any free services, discounts or gratuities, nor in any
other way confers any benefit upon me. I am not an employee of Todd
Aero. I do not perform aircraft maintenance for hire. I am not an officer or
director of Todd Aero. I use both my (leaseback) airplane and Robert
Todd's airplane for instruction and scenic flights, as do other instructors.
My personal income related to using these airplanes is in excess of
$1,000 annually. This income is from my students and scenic flight
customers. I have no other business dealing with Robert Todd or Todd
Aero.

     2) Sierra Warbirds, Inc.: Because they do not offer flight instruction, I have
no relationship other than exchanging pleasantries.

     3) Pilot Services Corporation: I have used its airplane on a few occasions
for instruction and scenic flights in the past three and a half years. Pilot
Services, Inc. has upon rare occasion, asked me to do a scenic flight (in
its airplane) for its customer. Pilot Services Corporation and I have a
verbal agreement that I may use its airplane for flight instruction and
scenic flights. Pilot Services Corporation has offered me use of office
space to work with students, however I have not felt the need to use the
space. I am not an officer, director or employee of Pilot Services
Corporation, and I have no other business dealings with it. Pilot Services
Corporation does not refer students to me because it has its own

                                                                                                    —2—

instructor doing its instruction. My personal income from students and
scenic flight customers using the Pilot Services Corporation airplane is
less than $100 annually.

4) Generally speaking, when a flight instructor aligns himself/herself
with an FBO, he/she flies only that FBO's airplanes (or customers'
personal airplanes). However, at a small airport where people know
each other, it is not uncommon to use another FBO's airplane on
occasion. I primarily use Todd Aero's aircraft because there is more
flexibility in scheduling of two airplanes instead of one, Todd Aero
maintains a friendlier atmosphere than Pilot Services Corporation, and I
know and trust the maintenance performed on Todd Aero airplanes
whereas I do not know who maintains the Pilot Services Corporation
airplane.

5) The rental rate for a Todd Aero Cessna 172 is $51 per hour or $480 for
a 10-hour block of time. The rental rate is the same for both of the Cessna
172s that Todd Aero uses (and was the same as the third plane Todd
Aero had on leaseback in the summer of 1992). I encourage my students,
and others who fly frequently, to purchase block time in order to reduce
their flying costs.

h) 5/23/89       I briefed the TTAD board that I had contacted the FPPC
regarding using Todd Aero airplanes for instructional and
paid scenic flight purposes, and had been told that there is
no conflict of interest in my using Todd Aero airplanes. I do
not know the date of that telephone conversation nor the
name of the person with whom I spoke, but I was definitely
left with the impression that there was no conflict of interest.
The FPPC person sent me sections of part 18700 regarding
material decisions.

i) 5/14/90       My wife and I purchased a Cessna 172, registration number
N61710, and placed it on leaseback with Todd Aero. The
leaseback is an oral agreement under which Todd Aero
pays for fuel, maintenance and insurance, and charges a
$5 per hour flown administrative fee. Todd Aero gets a
discount on fuel purchased from TTAD. When my wife or I
fly the airplane for personal use, we buy the fuel from TTAD
without receiving a fuel discount. If my wife or I fly the
airplane for our personal use Todd Aero charges us the
administrative fee. If the hourly rental fees exceed the costs,
Todd Aero issues me a check at the end of the month. If the
rental fees do not exceed the costs, I issue Todd Aero a
check. See ENCL (1) for an example of how a monthly
statement might typically appear. This aircraft is used by me
and other instructors for flight instruction and scenic flights

                                                                                    —3—

as well as rental to qualified pilots. As a leaseback, the
airplane earns me payment (from Todd Aero) in excess of
$1,000 annually.

j) 6/20/90

My wife and I made a personal loan to Mr. Robert Todd (the
sole proprietor of Todd Aero) of $10,000 for the purpose of
his purchasing a Cessna 172, registration number N9110H.
The reason for his purchasing the airplane was to use it for
rental in his business. I have enclosed a copy of the loan
agreement as ENCL (2). The loan is secured by Robert
Todd's Cessna 172, N9110H. This aircraft is used by me
and other instructors for flight instruction and scenic flights,
as well as rental to qualified pilots. I do not fly this airplane
for personal use as I would have to pay the regular rental
for rate. I earn in excess of $1,000 (P & I) each year from the
loan, and will until it is paid in full on 5/31/95.

k) 11/3/92

I was re-elected to the TTAD Board of Directors

l) 11/19/92

I visited the FPPC office in Sacramento and talked with a
staff attorney (I believe his name is Warren) to discuss the
possibility of my having a conflict of interest due to using
Todd Aero airplanes for instructing. He did not indicate that I
have a conflict of interest, nor did he indicate that I do not.
He did give me more sections of Part 18700 and two
handouts on "Conflicts of Interest Under the Political Reform
Act" and "California's Conflict of Interest Law for Public
Officials."

m) 12/15/92

At the TTAD regular board meeting we determined that one
board member believes that I have a conflict of interest in
TTAD decisions regarding Todd Aero, Sierra Warbirds, Inc.,
and Pilot Services Corporation, and that I do not believe I
do. My belief is based on answering the four questions
(taken from "California's Conflict of Interest Law for Public
Officials") used in determining whether an official has a
conflict of interest. The board's action on this item was that I
would write to the FPPC asking advice and , until that
advice is received, I will not participate in discussion on,
attempt to influence, or participate in a TTAD decision on
any of the three FBOs.

The foregoing information and statements have been made willingly. The
advice which I am requesting from the FPPC is whether or not I have a conflict
of interest in TTAD decisions regarding Todd Aero, Sierra Warbirds, Inc., and/or
Pilot Services Corporation If you would like me to come to Sacramento for an

-4-

interview, I would be happy to do that. My home telephone number is (916) 587-7281.

Please send all correspondence regarding this matter to:
Mr. Mark Foxwell
TTAD General Manager
P.O. Box 125
Truckee CA 96160-0125

AND

Mr. John Phelps, Esq.
Porter, Simon, Graham, Phelps & Cuttitta
40200 Truckee Airport Rd.
Truckee CA, 96161


Thank you,

[signature]

Thomas A. Meadows
Director

cc: John Phelps
Mark Foxwell

ENCL (1)

Sample generic monthly leaseback statement

1. Income:
   Total flight hours: 32
   i.) flight hours X $51 (regular rate): 12 hrs.                612
   ii.) flight hours X $48 (block time rate): 18 hrs.            864
   iii.) maint/owner hours: 2                                     00

2. Total income:                                                 1476

3. Expenses:
   i.) TTAD fuel:                           280
   ii.) outside fuel:                        38
   iii.) maint: invoice 0123               476
   iv.) insurance                          295
   v.) administrative fee (32X$5)          160

4. Total expenses:                                               1249

5. Total due owner/ <due FBO>                                    227


** These figures are for illustrative purposes only and are not factual
for any given month.

ENCL (2)


1. Robert O. Todd, on JUNE 20, 1990, for value received, promise
to pay to Thomas or Lynn Meadows, or order, the sum of 10,000
dollars in lawful money of the United States, with interest
thereon in like lawful money, from AUGUST 31,1990
until paid at the rate of EIGHT per cent per ANNUM said
interest herewith six principals. If said interest is not paid as
it becomes due it is to be added to the principal sum and become
a part thereof, and thereafter bear interest at the same rate.

Payments to be made are $608.20 each quarter, due and payable on:
AUGUST 31, NOVEMBER 30, FEBRUARY 28, and MAY 31 of each year. The
term of this note is five years.

And as collateral, security for the payment of the above note,
interest thereon and expenses that may accrue concerning same,
Robert O. Todd has released the following property, to wit:
Cessna 172N Serial Number 172851181 N3110H

And should the said note, or any part thereof, or interesthereon,
remain unpaid after it should have been paid, according to the
terms of said note, I Robert O. Todd hereby irrevocably authorize
said Thomas of Lynn Meadows or their assigns, to sell the above
described property at public or private sale, with or without
previous demand or demand for performance of the terms of said
note, and with or without previous notice to Robert O. Todd of such
sale, and apply the proceeds thereof in payment of the costs and
charges of such sale, all interest due upon said note and the
unpaid principal; the balance, if any, to be paid to Mike Thomas
or his representative. Upon demand.

In case of fall in the market value of any of said securities,
Robert O. Todd promises to reduce the amount of said note, or to
increase the security in proportion to such decrease of value in
default of which said note shall be considered and treated is
immediately due and payable, anything in said note tothe contrary
notwithstanding. In case of the payment of the above note,
principal and interest, according to the terms therefor, the above
described securities shall be returned to Robert O. Todd.


Robert O. Todd
ROBERT O. TODD

Thomas Meadows
THOMAS MEADOWS

Lynn Meadows
LYNN MEADOWS
